content.claim: Hungary's 2013 centralization via the Public Education Act transferred school management, budgeting, staffing and curriculum control from municipalities to the central Klebelsberg Institution Maintenance Centre (KLIK), eliminating the municipal bargaining power and budget autonomy that most European closure-prevention mechanisms presume
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Centralization is a structural constraint documented post-hoc; no comparative study directly measures how KLIK centralization altered closure patterns relative to pre-2013 municipality-driven decisions
Contemporary reporting on KLIK's scope confirms structural change but does not quantify the effect on rural school retention or closure trajectories
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
